key_insight: This survey frames large language models as a new interface layer for vehicle routing problems, enabling natural-language modeling, heuristic design, and tool-coordinated optimization.
engineering_relevance: It is relevant for logistics and industrial scheduling because it can help translate real operational requirements into solvable routing pipelines with constraints like capacity, time windows, and pickups and deliveries.
method: Reviews VRP fundamentals, major variants, and solver families.
Organizes LLM approaches into three roles: modelers, designers, and coordinators.
Covers benchmarks, operational datasets, evaluation frameworks, and comparative experiments.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the survey includes standard benchmarks, real or near-real datasets, LLM-oriented evaluation frameworks, and two comparative experiments.
watch_item: LLM outputs still need careful validation because routing problems are NP-hard and constraint violations or tool-integration errors can make generated solutions unreliable.
paper_title: Vehicle Routing Problem Meets Large Language Models: An Overview and Perspectives
